back_translation: And this is the blessing with which Moses the man of God blessed the sons of Israel before his death.
And he said: The Lord came from Sinai, and dawned from Seir to them; He shone forth from Mount Paran, and came from myriads of holiness; from His right hand, fire of law for them.
Surely the lover of peoples, all His holy ones are in Your hand; and they sat at Your feet, each receives from Your words.
A law Moses commanded to us, a possession of the assembly of Jacob.
And there was in Jeshurun a king, when the heads of the people gathered, together the tribes of Israel.
Let Reuben live and not die, and let his men be a number.
And this to Judah; and he said: Hear, O Lord, the voice of Judah, and to his people bring him; his hands are sufficient for him, and a help against his foes may You be.
And to Levi he said: Your Thummim and Your Urim be with the man of Your godly one, whom You tested at Massah, with whom You contested at the waters of Meribah,
the one saying of his father and of his mother: I have not seen him; and his brothers he did not recognize, and his sons he did not know, for they kept Your word and Your covenant they will guard.
They will teach Your ordinances to Jacob and Your law to Israel; they will place incense in Your nostrils, and whole burnt offering on Your altar.
Bless, O Lord, his substance, and the work of his hands accept; smash the loins of those who rise against him, and of those who hate him, that they may not rise.
To Benjamin he said: The beloved of the Lord shall dwell securely upon Him; covering over him all the day, and between His shoulders he dwells.
And to Joseph he said: Blessed by the Lord be his land, with the precious things of the heavens, with the dew, and from the deep lying beneath,
and with the precious things of the produce of the sun, and with the precious things of the yield of the moons,
and from the chief things of the ancient mountains, and with the precious things of the everlasting hills,
and with the precious things of the earth and its fullness, and the favor of Him who dwelt in the bush; let them come to the head of Joseph, and to the crown of the prince among his brothers.

The firstborn of his ox—majesty is to him, and the horns of a wild ox are his horns; with them peoples he will gore, together, to the ends of the earth; and they are the myriads of Ephraim, and they are the thousands of Manasseh.
And to Zebulun he said: Rejoice, O Zebulun, in your going out, and Issachar, in your tents.
Peoples to a mountain they will call; there they will sacrifice sacrifices of righteousness, for the abundance of the seas they will suck, and the hidden treasures of the sand.
And to Gad he said: Blessed is the one who enlarges Gad; like a lioness he dwells, and tears the arm, even the crown.
And he saw the first part for himself, for there the portion of a lawgiver was reserved; and he came with the heads of the people, the righteousness of the Lord he did, and His judgments with Israel.
And to Dan he said: Dan is a lion's cub; he leaps from Bashan.
And to Naphtali he said: Naphtali is satisfied with favor, and full of the blessing of the Lord; the sea and the south he shall possess.
And to Asher he said: Blessed of sons is Asher; may he be the favored of his brothers, and dipping his foot in oil.
Iron and bronze are your bolts, and like your days is your strength.
There is none like God, O Jeshurun, the rider of the heavens to your help, and in His majesty the clouds.
A dwelling place is the God of antiquity, and underneath are arms of eternity; and He drove out from before you the enemy, and said: Destroy!
And Israel dwelt securely alone, the fountain of Jacob, to a land of grain and new wine; also his heavens drip dew.

Blessed are you, O Israel! Who is like you, a people saved in the Lord, the shield of your help, and who is the sword of your majesty; and your enemies shall lie down to you, and you shall tread upon their high places.
